component.name: Spin FM
component.summary: Lightweight tabbed file management for Linux

description.p: Spin FM is a compact Qt file manager with tabbed navigation, drag and drop, Trash support, configurable themes, removable USB device controls, and an embedded audio player.
Long-running copy, move, delete, storage, and device operations run outside the interface thread to keep navigation responsive.
Keeps every Trash-location row on the active application theme before selection, including USB and mounted-volume Trash.
Removes a duplicate Ctrl+T action so New Tab has one unambiguous window-level shortcut.
Adds a large full-path Trash chooser, keeps F9 mount actions visible with remembered sizing, defaults first-run icons to Adwaita, and tightens worker and cleanup lifecycles.
Makes Trash navigation and emptying explicit, changes local drag-and-drop into confirmed moves with conflict prompts, and documents the supported PyQt 5 source dependencies.
Adds native MPRIS desktop-player registration, complete wrapped names, LWM Dark and Graphite themes, and a simplified cache-free Debian build interface.
Makes Alt+P deterministic across delayed multimedia backends, adds routed audio shortcuts, and standardizes GPL-2.0-or-later release metadata.
Hardens cache-free Debian and source release automation and removes legacy artifacts.
Makes source, test, release, and Debian workflows cache-free by default and adds deterministic source-archive validation.
Removes Python-index packaging, modernizes direct Debian installation, and hardens optional Wayland_OSD discovery and failure isolation.
Restores double-click activation, expands the seekable audio player, and adds optional failure-isolated Wayland_OSD feedback.
Adds Ctrl+Up parent navigation and a lazy-loaded, theme-aware player that starts common audio files on activation.
Stability release with safe command launching, coalesced storage scans, rollback-safe overwrites, and expanded runtime tests.
Production-focused release with asynchronous operations, bounded caches, a refreshed interface, and Debian packaging.
